reviews.review_text:
i feel like i now have enough hours in this game to post my view

as it stands i will not currently be recommending this game to anyone based entirely on the fact that every update seems to be filled with broken promises like "400% server performance increase"

every single time a major update comes out it breaks more than one of the main features or components of the game for example... after the 1.29 update players would randomly die by simply logging out of the game, die to invisible zombies, die because you had to f11 yourself due to your character being bugged and showing low stats like no food or water, server kicking you from the game due to "bad connection" on the 400% performance increased servers, most animals make 0 noise so wolfs and bears are fun!, frame rate is now worse than it ever was even though this update was delayed time and time again so they could get it right.

my review of this game will change if the direction they take it also changes and by that i mean a direction where the game runs as intended...and lets not talk about what they done to xbox players!! but hey look a new bag and some furry shoes yaaaay

Love the game. Wish I could continue to play it, but the cheating is just rampant. It ruins what could really be something special. You basically have to choose between official servers, where there's a 50/50 chance a cheater will kill you through a wall, or the community servers, where the admins play god and give away your location to their buddies. What's wrong with people? I will never understand how playing a game like that could be fun or rewarding. Oh well.

A game with no storymode, yet so many stories —
enemies, betrayals, campfires, shots, words, beginnings, ends, goodbyes, laughs, memories, truths, lies, cold nights, warm days, and friends.
footsteps in the dark, broken promises, empty cans, full backpacks, bleeding wounds, stitched arms, loaded mags, silent nods, roadside graves, sudden storms, lucky escapes, hopeless odds, shared meals.

No other game will have you remember you're time with friends better than this game. 
Camping in the woods at night with a fire for light and warmth talking about life and plans.
I will forever remember this game for its peaceful side and how fast it can all change.

As someone who fell in love with this game during the days of the ARMA 2 mod, it genuinely breaks my heart that I have just played it for the last time. I simply cannot recommend this game with the rampant hacking scene that plagues it. 

At its core, DayZ is honestly one of the most unique experiences in gaming, no other game comes close for me. But spending days building up a character just to drop dead in the middle of a forest with no one around isn't a fun time.

It was manageable in the past but it's utterly lost to the hackers now. What a shame.
Beautiful walking simulator with occasional jump scares. 100% recommend

Great community, interesting game, amazing overall and honestly a great recommendation for everyone to try at least once.

I have played over 5000+ hours and I love the game a lot. The PVP scene personally is not my thing. I tried it for a couple hundred hours across multiple PVP servers and it just wasn’t for me. But if we talk about PVE, I can tell you the PVE servers are amazing. There are tons of amazing PVE servers with great communities and a lot of really fun experiences.

The game becomes even more fun with modded servers.

You want to fight Aliens? Sure, jump into futuristic modded servers and get your sci-fi experience.

You want hardcore survival? Tons of servers have brutal hardcore elements and survival mechanics.

You want RPG-style gameplay in DayZ? A lot of servers have skill menus, EXP grinding, progression systems, immersive mods, quests and really interactive mechanics that completely change the gameplay experience in a fun way.

DayZ is one of those games where every server can feel like a completely different game.

Amazing game overall. My honest two cents: INVITE friends. You will have an amazing time together. Whatever it is — jankiness, weird bugs, random moments — it somehow becomes part of the fun and creates unforgettable experiences.
hopped on build a base got raided then got brutaly raped by 5 guys and left i would recommend even tho its gives you depression.

Ah the game Dayz what can we say its like a 50 50 in between good and ♥♥♥♥ at the same time well the good part is you loot up fight raid live like a nomad ( Chad ) or be a base builder ( Chud )  and the negative side of it is getting killed by multiple Hackers ( ♥♥♥♥ )  On different Official servers  whether that might be from getting killed by speed hacks, Esp, Flying Cars and etc i am aswell a Console Dayz player as with 3314 hours to my name so i have been through a lot of ♥♥♥♥ with this game including many hacks on console aswell but do i still play this game yes do i love this game yes do i love the current devs no ( Because they are lazy and cant even add a real anti cheat to Pc/Console) so yes i do recommend this game to a extend if u have patience and you are stoic i think u will love this game but besides my opinion it is really a great survival game and the only reason why i still like this game even more is because its Czech (Miluji česky)  now u know a veterans opinion

You were warned.

This is not a finished game. IMHO I cannot and will not recommend this to anybody as the game is simply not something that should be listed under any circumstances as a finished product. The fact that there are so many things in it that are beyond broken and stupid and not working almost since the first time I played this which is a little more than 10 years ago, it is disgusting to see people recommending this as if its something that is simply works when it is not.

The fact that zombies, the whole AI system revolving around those zombies is totally broken, the fact that you  can run the game at high frames but it still feels like you are playing something that is running on 15 fps lets say, from movement jankiness, to many other glitching and breaking things that for a finished product SHOULD not be present in the game. From making absurd amounts of money for a product which is based around an game engine which is not  for this. The fact that you couldn't spend extra effort making the most out of those elements which the games soles purpose is based around is disappointing. But to each their own. I will not recommend this and in all honesty as a developer myself I cannot. Shameful to see the price tag and see someone living off of others and not being able to give their hundred percent to deliver a what you are so calling a finished game. More than 10 years have passed since this game didn't even have a software renderer, and the actual feel of the game is still pretty much the same. Disappointing and shameful.

Do you really think your piece of ♥♥♥♥, lazily developed pathetic little dumpsterfire of a glorified mod deserves the price tag, let alone the audacity to PERMANENTLY BAN steam family sharing? You developers are ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ pathetic.
get mercd as a fresh spawn 30 times in a row nobody wants to team up its just a pvp game with no real rewards for any effort, the games idea is cool the community sucks

DayZ is a special game. It's the game that got me into PC gaming, and even after all these years, there really isn't anything else quite like it.

The idea alone was crazy to me when I first played it: a massive open-world map, no hand-holding, and one simple objective—spawn, find loot, and survive by any means necessary.

What makes DayZ so special is that no two sessions are ever the same. Every time you log in, you're creating a new story. Sometimes that story ends five minutes after spawning because someone snipes you while you're crossing a field. Other times you run into another survivor, have a conversation, team up, and spend hours traveling across the map together.

You might spend a night building a base, raiding another group's compound, hunting for supplies, helping fresh spawns, or getting into a firefight that you'll remember for years. The game constantly creates memorable moments through both player interactions and the world itself.

The best part is that you never truly know what's going to happen next. Every person you meet could become a friend, an enemy, or the reason your adventure takes an unexpected turn.

DayZ is one of the most immersive survival games ever made. It's frustrating, tense, unpredictable, and sometimes brutally unfair, but that's exactly what makes the experience so memorable.

It's a game I'll never forget, and one that continues to hold a special place in my heart.

Yes. i find this game to be more enjoyable now, thanks to the fact of i can effectively run a game server and the game itself on one rig, and not have to worry about bs players ruining my survival run just cause they like to PVP for no reason. 

Although, the server does kind of get annoying fixing yourself after a while, but in the end its worth the headache to enjoy a game like this.

8/10 stars, would buy again if possible

I remember when I first started playing this game I saw it was highly recommended at first I didn't get it and I didn't like it i was like what is the point of this I logged off after my first day however that night i was like okay ill give it another shot tomorrow and let me tell you that was the beginning of an exciting adventure I play this game so much now yeah sometimes you live for a long time sometimes you die really quickly id have to say this is the most realistic zombie open world survival game I've ever played that's what has kept me hooked for so long and I will continue to play get better and just have fun so fun to meet strangers as well. I've gotten to the point that I am now making my own server and adding mods with the workshop I can play on my own private server when i just want it to be me and the zombies as well or if i just want to explore and see places I've never been too. This game is so wonderful yet painful as well especially if you die to something dumb but that the beauty of it all you get to start over again and its quite refreshing sometimes its more fun to work your way up from nothing and to do it again or give your gear to a freshie and start over. the friends I've made even gotten my wife to play with me as well which is so fun this game will always be on my pc because yeah I take my breaks time to time however when I play I'm playing for hours. servers are packed and they are releasing a new map I'm so excited yet scared cause change can suck could be great however I just hope nobody does anything to ruin this already beautiful game. overall I love this game and it is my favorite game to play.

I started playing DayZ Standalone in 2013 when it first launched as early access. I really loved the concept but damn it was far from perfect in implementation. I also played the mod for Arma 2 and remember it fondly. DayZ Standalone made the horror survival game its focus and went all in. The adventure was there, the human interaction element was there, the PvE with the infected was pretty rough but was there. I have played the game on and off ever since, I have phases of gaming and getting busy with life lol. But I have watched DayZ grow and really create a community around the world, of people who love this hardcore survival game. The mechanics and physics are a bit dated now, the infected still have their issues and are pretty stiff in animations, the gun-play is fun but I would say it needs some updating for a more realistic feel. The thing that has really kept DayZ going is the modding scene. You can flip through the server list and find servers that completely change the gameplay to something that feels like an entirely different game. You have the choice to play PvP servers or PvE if you prefer more exploration. The amount of modded maps that are available are also noteworthy, as some maps have quest-lines to obtain rare and powerful loot that only spawns on said map. The ability to customize your experience to this level is S tier. Looking forward to the future of the game and next iterations.

Do Not Buy This Game

The fix, of the fix, of the fix, of the fix, etc... never really addresses the bugs that this game continues to be rife with, even after more than eight years of 'fixing it'.
The most recent 'glitch' has been the "inventory access dance" since the release of v1.19.
Standing directly in front of three containers, you may have access to one, two, three, or none, of them.
Looking through Bohemia Interactive's bug reporting site, you see this mentioned, but they are all marked as Duplicates. Without of course being able to find the original.
My rented server still had three months left on it when v1.19 released, and I have become too frustrated with trying to access my storage to enjoy playing since. I would love to get that wasted money for that from Bohemia Interactive for the immersion breaking glitch they introduced with this patch.

I own all three Armas and Dayz, but with this kind of glitch fumbling, I will never buy another Bohemia Interactive game again.

for a "survival" game, the devs do literal nothing in the way of putting things in the game to actually survive. food, water, decent clothes to keep warm in the nothing but sub zero temperatures are next to impossible to find. youre lucky to find a can of dog food every three towns you go to and if your character gets sick (which is guaranteed to happen 100% of the time) youre not gonna find any meds in the game to help. youre lucky to find vitamins in one of ten settlements you visit and spend hours searching in each. health and blood regen is honestly slower than any other game ive played with the same mechanic. surviving simply isnt possible without a ton of mods but to have those you gotta have whats in a public server....a server with everything already raided and totally empty. and unless you have a second pc you cant make your own private server for yourself and friends to prevent the possibility of griefers or troll players. the negatives out weigh the positives on a biblical scale in this game. its ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, the fact i wasted my money on a game thats next to impossible to survive in pisses me off and it ruins any kind of enjoyment for those that want to get into free roam survival zombie games. CoD Cold War zombies outbreak is a several steps above this. 7 days to die is better than this. DEAD ISLAND IS BETTER THAN THIS!!!! FIX THE ♥♥♥♥!!!

At the core the game is amazing. Love the adrenaline and slow paced nature of the game. However the official servers are plagued with hackers that the devs refuse to address properly. And the the communities response is "dOn'T pLAy oFfiCiaL". Unacceptable. With the nature of how long it takes to gear and base build, you are going to basically be playing loot/walk simulator. 

Example: Top of a building in a small room with ZERO windows and one way in via ladder. Randomly falls over and dies with no sounds. Comes back thinking it was a death by de-sync only to find a dead fully geared player at the door of my base (ground level) to then fall over and die again the second I enter the combination to the lock.

DayZ is not a game. It’s a lifestyle choice. It’s a PhD in suffering, with a minor in paranoia.

You spawn with nothing but a glow stick and a dream, and the game immediately reminds you that dreams are fragile things. Hunger gnaws, thirst mocks, and zombies… well, they’re the least of your problems. The real monsters are the players, each one a psychology experiment with legs. Free will in DayZ is like handing toddlers flamethrowers: fascinating, terrifying, and occasionally hilarious.

The difficulty curve? Imagine climbing Everest, blindfolded, while juggling rotten peaches. But once you get it—once you learn that canned beans are more valuable than gold, that trust is a currency rarer than ammo—the game transforms. Every victory feels earned, every betrayal feels Shakespearean, and every weird encounter becomes a story you’ll retell forever.

DayZ is unpredictable theater. One moment you’re sharing a fire with a stranger, the next you’re being force‑fed disinfectant by the same guy. It’s cruel, it’s absurd, it’s beautiful. And after 1474 hours, I can confidently say: I still don’t know if I’m playing DayZ, or if DayZ is playing me.

After years as a console player, I've finally made the leap to PC—and I couldn't be happier. As someone passionate about game development, the creative tools and freedom on PC are incredible. No more editing .json and .xml files on a mobile device; now I can fully dive into creating detailed maps and complex mods. I love building custom servers and crafting mod files that console players can enjoy, especially since mod support there is so limited. Adding dynamic spawns to existing maps is a favorite challenge, and I’ve even tweaked zombie spawns to feel more realistic—no more enemies magically appearing right in front of you. This platform has unlocked a whole new level of creativity for me. 10/10, I absolutely love this game and the possibilities it offers.

game went to abysmal ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ stage with the latest 1.29. all bohemia does is grabbing mod content and putting it into the game, then selling new dlcs and raise the base game price. what studio introduces more regressions than actual fixes in an update? and dont hope for dayz2, the day it releases it will also be outdated and bugged as hell because this game studio is ran by goblins.

I heard they had a cheater problem but I've only encountered one ever on xbox and pc with over 400+ hours total.
Animals will scare you if your brain's on autpilot mode.
Nighttime is terrefying.
You WILL find 70 gun parts for a specific gun that you will NEVER find no matter how much you search and play the game. You can visit every high tier loot zone, every helicopter crash and ever toxic zone on the map and still not find the gun of which you found parts for at the start of your playthrhrough.
The plus side is that when you  win a proper gunfight it feels very rewarding.
